mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate robust aggregate demand and economic momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in the index of industrial production following a contraction reflects rapid supply-side recovery and capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during recovery phases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 230.0 indicate entrenched inflation that forces aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of high-inflation triggered instances, or signal predictive power vanishes after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports consumer confidence and corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Interaction signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or multivariate model shows negative coefficient for the joint labor indicator.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects strong underlying economic activity and corporate profit expansion, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with quarterly GDP growth (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 90.0 signals severe manufacturing contraction and supply chain bottlenecks that suppress corporate output and earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 45%.
